reviewer: Kimi native-size reference review (DEMON FIST build: five authored blocks, shared integrator, prowl orbit + universal drift, elite warn invulnerability, super armor, heavy hyper-armor, adrenaline rally wheel; montage re-inspected at 160x360 against both references plus native crops of the opening/market pair, the jab/launcher/slam/sweep strip, the counter/gate-warn/gate-land/mob/super strip, and the rendered 30s clip frame at 8s)

characterCraft.note: The fighter is a constructed ~11x25 white-gi brawler at reference scale: red headband with fluttering tails, black belt, and one oversized cursed right fist (dark red with ember cracks) that carries every attack pose — jab-chain extension, launcher uppercut glow, low sweep spin, lean-back dodge, airborne slam dive, and the GOD WHEEL roulette with three afterimage fists. The four street demons read at a glance: jacket-and-cap thug, lean mint sprinter, bare-chested mohawk bruiser with grey armor plates that visibly crack off, and horned purple demon with ember eyes and white claws. The GATEKEEPER elite is bigger, crowned in gold, with a raised-claw windup. Windup tells live on the bodies (pulled-back fist, overhead raise, ember gape), facing tracks actual travel through the shared integrator (zero wrong-way/crab violations in the behavior suite), and KO'd enemies tumble out under dizzy stars.

environmentCraft.note: The five blocks are built, not palette-swapped, with four parallax layers plus a near foreground plane. BACK ALLEY is night bricks with fire-escape zigzags, drainpipes, a flickering FIST neon blade with a glow pool, dumpsters, and a lit distant rooftop row. MARKET ROOF runs a sunset with sun disc and cloud streaks, striped awnings, sagging lantern strings with glow pools, water tanks, vents and stall clutter. DOCK SIDE has crane silhouettes, a crescent moon, corrugated container stacks, bollards, and water shimmering past the dock edge. OLD DOJO is warm dusk with pagoda roofs, maple canopies, shoji screens in warm grids, hanging paper lanterns, and a beam lattice. DEMON GATE is obsidian spikes, hanging chains and chain curtains, ember gape windows, a horned gate with glowing eyes, lava seams that breathe, and rising ash. The street stays a quiet stage: long seams and tone bands, no speckle fields (the executable ceilings pass).

levelVariety.note: Across the fifteen-beat sheet the wall grammar, side silhouettes, material identity and composition change per block — wet neon alley, warm market dusk, teal dock night, lantern-lit dojo, ember hell-gate — and the executable structure-distance gate holds across all block pairs (measured 0.35-0.41, well over the 0.16 floor). Hazards re-skin the same fight grammar honestly: the elite walks in under the DEMON GATE banner in any block, and the mob flanks from both edges.

animationImpact.note: Aligned fighter and gatekeeper bursts pass their motion floors. The jab chain walks forward through enemies (aggressor-owns-the-space separation), the launcher pops the bruiser airborne with an ARMOR CRACKED beat and gold star decal, the slam spikes the demon into the dirt with a crater decal and white ring, the sweep drops the whole cluster with a spinning low pose, the counter backsteps exactly on the enemy's raised-fist tell, GOD WHEEL spins with three afterimages, a gold ring and shockwaves, and the finale lands a full celebration with confetti and the DEMON GATE card. Payoff-diff crops vs same-sim __NO_PAYOFF_FX twins confirm sparks, decals and pulses land on the actors.

readability.note: Intent reads from the bodies alone: honest facing that tracks travel, the raised-fist windup tells (bigger on bruisers, demons, and the crowned keeper), the elite's physical march-in, and the mob's two-edge flanking — zero drawn guidelines anywhere (opposite forced plans render byte-identical at five beats). Good and bad beats are palette-separated: gold stars, mint cracks, cyan counters and gold pulses for launchers/juggles/counters/combo milestones/elite KOs/mob clears/supers; coral flashes, shatter decals and coral pulses for hits taken, combo drops and knockdowns — all proven by the executable feedback suite (50 sampled beats, all palette-separated at the event location, thinnest 18px). HP, meter, combo count, and the five block pips sit in a compact bottom cluster beside the fighter (pinned by an executable status-near-fighter gate), and the street flows left with travel (executable scroll-coherence gate).

artDirectionCohesion.note: The dusk-city arc of the strip stays one world: ink nights, amber horizons, neon magenta, warm dojo paper, teal dock water, and ember red — gold always means won, coral always means hurt. The pixel construction, the DEMON FIST identity strip, the tactic readout, the broadcast banner, the payoff decal language, and the five block palettes read as one authored street, exactly like the references.

guidelineOverlays.note: Checked all fifteen sampled beats at native size: no route lines/dots, arrows, target highlights, intercept predictions, ghost phantoms, predicted arcs, or safe-lane markers anywhere. The keeper's march, the mob's flanking, and the DEMON GATE banner are physical world telegraphs; windup tells live on the actors. Executable pixel gates (opposite forced plans at five beats) render identically, and the banned overlay primitives are absent from source.
